sourceQuestion:
An ant walks in a straight line on a plane leaving behind a trace of its movement. The initial position of the ant is at point P facing east. The ant first turns 72 degrees anticlockwise at P, and then does the following two steps in sequence exactly FIVE times before halting.

1. moves forward for 10 cms
2. turns 144 degrees clockwise

The pattern made by the trace left behind by the ant is

A. [regular pentagon]
B. [irregular star]
C. [regular 5-pointed star / pentagram]
D. [open spiral]

parsed.stem:
An ant walks in a straight line on a plane leaving behind a trace of its movement. The initial position of the ant is at point P facing east. The ant first turns 72 degrees anticlockwise at P, and then does the following two steps in sequence exactly FIVE times before halting.

1. moves forward for 10 cms
2. turns 144 degrees clockwise

The pattern made by the trace left behind by the ant is

sourceQuestion:
Ms. X came out of a building through its front door to find her shadow due to the morning sun falling to her right side with the building to her back. From this, it can be inferred that the building is facing _________.

A. North  B. East  C. West  D. South

parsed.stem: Ms. X came out of a building through its front door to find her shadow due to the morning sun falling to her right side with the building to her back. From this, it can be inferred that the building is facing _________.

sourceQuestion:
The front door of Mr. X's house faces East. Mr. X leaves the house, walking 50m straight from the back door that is situated directly opposite to the front door. He then turns to his right, walks for another 50m and stops. The direction of the point Mr. X is now located at with respect to the starting point is _______.

A. South-East  B. North-East  C. West  D. North-West

parsed.stem: The front door of Mr. X's house faces East. Mr. X leaves the house, walking 50m straight from the back door that is situated directly opposite to the front door. He then turns to his right, walks for another 50m and stops. The direction of the point Mr. X is now located at with respect to the starting point is _______.

sourceQuestion:
X is 1 km northeast of Y. Y is 1 km southeast of Z. W is 1 km west of Z. P is 1 km south of W. Q is 1 km east of P. What is the distance between X and Q in km?

A. 1  B. sqrt(2)  C. sqrt(3)  D. 2

parsed.stem: X is 1 km northeast of Y. Y is 1 km southeast of Z. W is 1 km west of Z. P is 1 km south of W. Q is 1 km east of P. What is the distance between X and Q in km?

sourceQuestion:
Mr. Vivek walks 6 meters North-east, then turns and walks 6 meters South-east, both at 60 degrees to east. He further moves 2 meters South and 4 meters West. What is the straight distance in meters between the point he started from and the point he finally reached?

A. 2*sqrt(2)  B. 2  C. sqrt(2)  D. 1/sqrt(2)

parsed.stem: Mr. Vivek walks 6 meters North-east, then turns and walks 6 meters South-east, both at 60 degrees to east. He further moves 2 meters South and 4 meters West. What is the straight distance in meters between the point he started from and the point he finally reached?

sourceQuestion:
M and N start from the same location. M travels 10 km East and then 10 km North-East. N travels 5 km South and then 4 km South-East. What is the shortest distance (in km) between M and N at the end of their travel?

A. 18.00  B. 22.50  C. 20.61  D. 25.00

parsed.stem: M and N start from the same location. M travels 10 km East and then 10 km North-East. N travels 5 km South and then 4 km South-East. What is the shortest distance (in km) between M and N at the end of their travel?

sourceQuestion:
Fatima starts from point P, goes North for 3 km, and then East for 4 km to reach point Q. She then turns to face point P and goes 5 km in that direction. She then goes North for 8 km. How far is she from point P, and in which direction should she go to reach point P?

A. 8 km, North
B. 12 km, North
C. 6 km, North
D. 10 km, North

parsed.stem: Fatima starts from point P, goes North for 3 km, and then East for 4 km to reach point Q. She then turns to face point P and goes 5 km in that direction. She then goes North for 8 km. How far is she from point P, and in which direction should she go to reach point P?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the relationships among P, Q, R, S, and T:
- P is the brother of Q.
- S is the daughter of Q.
- R is the sister of Q.
- T is the mother of Q.

The following statements are made based on the relationships given above.
1. R is the grandmother of S.
2. R is the sister of T.
3. R has only one son.
4. Q has only one daughter.

Which one of the following options is correct?

A. Both (1) and (2) are true.
B. Both (1) and (3) are true.
C. Only (3) is true.
D. Only (4) is true.

1. R is the grandmother of S.
2. R is the sister of T.
3. R has only one son.
4. Q has only one daughter.

Which one of the following options is correct?

sourceQuestion:
Statement: Either P marries Q or X marries Y.

Among the options below, the logical NEGATION of the above statement is:

A. P does not marry Q and X marries Y.
B. Neither P marries Q nor X marries Y.
C. P does not marry Q and X does not marry Y.
D. P marries Q and X marries Y.
sourceAnswer: B. Neither P marries Q nor X marries Y.

parsed.stem:
Statement: Either P marries Q or X marries Y.

Among the options below, the logical NEGATION of the above statement is:

options.text: P does not marry Q and X marries Y.
Neither P marries Q nor X marries Y.
P does not marry Q and X does not marry Y.
P marries Q and X marries Y.

sourceQuestion:
Given the statements:
- P is the sister of Q.
- Q is the husband of R.
- R is the mother of S.
- T is the husband of P.

Based on the above information, T is ____________ of S.

A. the grandfather
B. an uncle
C. the father
D. a brother

Given the statements:
- P is the sister of Q.
- Q is the husband of R.
- R is the mother of S.
- T is the husband of P.

sourceQuestion:
M has a son Q and a daughter R. He has no other children. E is the mother of P and daughter-in-law of M. How is P related to M?

A. P is the son-in-law of M.
B. P is the grandchild of M.
C. P is the daughter-in-law of M.
D. P is the grandfather of M.
sourceAnswer: B. P is the grandchild of M.

parsed.stem: M has a son Q and a daughter R. He has no other children. E is the mother of P and daughter-in-law of M. How is P related to M?

options.text: P is the son-in-law of M.
P is the grandchild of M.
P is the daughter-in-law of M.
P is the grandfather of M.

sourceQuestion:
Each of P, Q, R, S, W, X, Y, and Z has been married at most once. X and Y are married and have two children P and Q. Z is the grandfather of the daughter of P. Further, W and Z are married and are parents of R. Which one of the following must necessarily be FALSE?

A. X is the mother-in-law of R.
B. P and R are not married to each other.
C. P is a son of X and Y.
D. Q cannot be married to R.
sourceAnswer: D. Q cannot be married to R.

parsed.stem: Each of P, Q, R, S, W, X, Y, and Z has been married at most once. X and Y are married and have two children P and Q. Z is the grandfather of the daughter of P. Further, W and Z are married and are parents of R. Which one of the following must necessarily be FALSE?

options.text: X is the mother-in-law of R.
P and R are not married to each other.
P is a son of X and Y.
Q cannot be married to R.

sourceQuestion:
P, Q, R, S, and T are related and belong to the same family. P is the brother of S. Q is the wife of P. R and S are the children of the siblings Q and T respectively. Which one of the following statements is necessarily FALSE?

A. S is the aunt of R.
B. T is the aunt of R.
C. Q is the sister-in-law of S.
D. S is the brother of T.
sourceAnswer: B. T is the aunt of R.

parsed.stem: P, Q, R, S, and T are related and belong to the same family. P is the brother of S. Q is the wife of P. R and S are the children of the siblings Q and T respectively. Which one of the following statements is necessarily FALSE?

options.text: S is the aunt of R.
T is the aunt of R.
Q is the sister-in-law of S.
S is the brother of T.

sourceQuestion:
M and N had four children P, Q, R, and S. Of them, only P and R were married. They had children X and Y respectively. If Y is a legitimate child of W, which of the following statements is necessarily FALSE?

A. M is the grandmother of Y.
B. R is the father of Y.
C. W is the wife of R.
D. W is the wife of P.
sourceAnswer: D. W is the wife of P.

parsed.stem: M and N had four children P, Q, R, and S. Of them, only P and R were married. They had children X and Y respectively. If Y is a legitimate child of W, which of the following statements is necessarily FALSE?

options.text: M is the grandmother of Y.
R is the father of Y.
W is the wife of R.
W is the wife of P.

sourceQuestion:
In which of the following options will the expression P >= M -> P >= S be definitely true?

A. M >= S -> P >= S
B. M >= S -> P >= M
C. Q >= M -> P >= S
D. Q >= M -> P >= M

parsed.stem: In which of the following options will the expression P >= M -> P >= S be definitely true?

sourceQuestion:
Based only on the conversation below, identify the logically correct inference.

"Even if I had known that you were in the hospital, I would not have gone there to see you", Ramya told Josephine.

A. Ramya knew that Josephine was in the hospital.
B. Ramya did not know that Josephine was in the hospital.
C. Ramya and Josephine were once close friends; but now, they are not.
D. Josephine was in the hospital due to an injury to her leg.
sourceAnswer: B. Ramya did not know that Josephine was in the hospital.

parsed.stem:
Based only on the conversation below, identify the logically correct inference.

"Even if I had known that you were in the hospital, I would not have gone there to see you", Ramya told Josephine.

options.text: Ramya knew that Josephine was in the hospital.
Ramya did not know that Josephine was in the hospital.
Ramya and Josephine were once close friends; but now, they are not.
Josephine was in the hospital due to an injury to her leg.

sourceQuestion:
P and Q have been allotted a hostel room with two beds, a study table, and an almirah. P is an avid bird-watcher and wants to sit at the table and watch birds outside the window. Q does not mind that as long as his bed is close to the ceiling fan.

Which one of the following arrangements suits them the most?

A. Table near window, P's bed near fan, Q's bed away from fan
B. Table near window, Q's bed near fan, P's bed away from fan
C. Table away from window, Q's bed near fan, P's bed near fan
D. Table away from window, P's bed near fan, Q's bed away from fan
sourceAnswer: A. Table near window, P's bed near fan, Q's bed away from fan

parsed.stem:
P and Q have been allotted a hostel room with two beds, a study table, and an almirah. P is an avid bird-watcher and wants to sit at the table and watch birds outside the window. Q does not mind that as long as his bed is close to the ceiling fan.

Which one of the following arrangements suits them the most?

options.text: Table near window, P's bed near fan, Q's bed away from fan
Table near window, Q's bed near fan, P's bed away from fan
Table away from window, Q's bed near fan, P's bed near fan
Table away from window, P's bed near fan, Q's bed away from fan

sourceQuestion:
A building has several rooms and doors as shown in the top view of the building given below. The doors are closed initially. What is the minimum number of doors that need to be opened in order to go from the point P to the point Q?

A. 4  B. 3  C. 2  D. 1

parsed.stem: A building has several rooms and doors as shown in the top view of the building given below. The doors are closed initially. What is the minimum number of doors that need to be opened in order to go from the point P to the point Q?

sourceQuestion:
The head of newly formed government desires to appoint five of the six selected members P, Q, R, S, T and U to portfolios of Home, Power, Defense, Telecom, and Finance. U does not want any portfolio if S gets one of the five. T wants either Home or Finance or no portfolio. Q says that if S gets Power or Telecom, then she must get the other of the two. R says she must get one of the portfolios unless P gets one.

Assuming that everyone was satisfied, which among the following is a valid distribution of portfolios?

A. P:Home, Q:Power, R:Defense, S:Telecom, T:Finance
B. R:Home, S:Power, P:Defense, Q:Telecom, T:Finance
C. P:Home, R:Power, T:Defense, S:Telecom, Q:Finance
D. Q:Home, S:Power, T:Defense, R:Telecom, U:Finance
sourceAnswer: B. R:Home, S:Power, P:Defense, Q:Telecom, T:Finance

parsed.stem:
The head of newly formed government desires to appoint five of the six selected members P, Q, R, S, T and U to portfolios of Home, Power, Defense, Telecom, and Finance. U does not want any portfolio if S gets one of the five. T wants either Home or Finance or no portfolio. Q says that if S gets Power or Telecom, then she must get the other of the two. R says she must get one of the portfolios unless P gets one.

Assuming that everyone was satisfied, which among the following is a valid distribution of portfolios?

sourceQuestion:
There are three boxes. One contains apples, another contains oranges and the last one contains both apples and oranges. All three are known to be incorrectly labeled. If you are permitted to open just one box and then pull out and inspect only one fruit, which box would you open to determine the contents of all three boxes?

A. The box labeled 'Apples'
B. The box labeled 'Apples and Oranges'
C. The box labeled 'Oranges'
D. Cannot be determined
sourceAnswer: B. The box labeled 'Apples and Oranges'

parsed.stem: There are three boxes. One contains apples, another contains oranges and the last one contains both apples and oranges. All three are known to be incorrectly labeled. If you are permitted to open just one box and then pull out and inspect only one fruit, which box would you open to determine the contents of all three boxes?

sourceQuestion:
Six students P, Q, R, S, T and U, with distinct heights, compare their heights and make the following observations.

Observation I: S is taller than R.
Observation II: Q is the shortest of all.
Observation III: T is taller than only one student.
Observation IV: V is taller than T but is not the tallest.

The number of students that are taller than R is the same as the number of students shorter than T. Who is V?

A. T
B. R
C. S
D. P

parsed.stem:
Six students P, Q, R, S, T and U, with distinct heights, compare their heights and make the following observations.

Observation I: S is taller than R.
Observation II: Q is the shortest of all.
Observation III: T is taller than only one student.
Observation IV: V is taller than T but is not the tallest.

The number of students that are taller than R is the same as the number of students shorter than T. Who is V?

sourceQuestion:
A survey for a certain year found that 90% of pregnant women received medical care at least once before giving birth. Of these women, 80% received medical care from doctors, while 40% received medical care from other healthcare providers.

Given this information, which one of the following statements can be inferred with certainty?

A. More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
B. Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
C. More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
D. Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
sourceAnswer: A. More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.

parsed.stem:
A survey for a certain year found that 90% of pregnant women received medical care at least once before giving birth. Of these women, 80% received medical care from doctors, while 40% received medical care from other healthcare providers.

Given this information, which one of the following statements can be inferred with certainty?

options.text: More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
More than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.
Less than half of the pregnant women received medical care at least once from a doctor.

sourceQuestion:
After the inauguration of the new building, the Head of the Department (HoD) collated faculty preferences for office space. P wanted a room adjacent to the lab. Q wanted to be close to the lift. R wanted a view of the playground and S wanted a corner office.

Assuming that everyone was satisfied, which among the following floor maps is a possible allocation?

A. [Playground at top; row: R, Q, P, S with LAB below P and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
B. [Playground at top; row: R, Q, P, S with LAB below S and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
C. [Playground at top; row: R, Q, S, P with LAB below P at edge corner and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
D. [Playground at top; row: S, Q, P, R with LAB below P and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
sourceAnswer: C. Layout with R facing playground, Q adjacent to lift, S in a corner, and P adjacent to the lab.

parsed.stem:
After the inauguration of the new building, the Head of the Department (HoD) collated faculty preferences for office space. P wanted a room adjacent to the lab. Q wanted to be close to the lift. R wanted a view of the playground and S wanted a corner office.

Assuming that everyone was satisfied, which among the following floor maps is a possible allocation?

options.text: [Playground at top; row: R, Q, P, S with LAB below P and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
[Playground at top; row: R, Q, P, S with LAB below S and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
[Playground at top; row: R, Q, S, P with LAB below P at edge corner and LIFT below Q; S is corner]
[Playground at top; row: S, Q, P, R with LAB below P and LIFT below Q; S is corner]

sourceQuestion:
In the square grid shown on the left, a person standing at position P2 is required to move to position P5. The only movement allowed for a step involves two moves along one direction followed by one move in a perpendicular direction (like a chess knight).

For example, a person at a given position X can only move to the positions marked X on the right. The permissible directions for movement are shown as dotted arrows in the right figure.

What is the minimum number of steps required to go from P2 to P5?

A. 4
B. 5
C. 6
D. 7

parsed.stem:
In the square grid shown on the left, a person standing at position P2 is required to move to position P5. The only movement allowed for a step involves two moves along one direction followed by one move in a perpendicular direction (like a chess knight).

For example, a person at a given position X can only move to the positions marked X on the right. The permissible directions for movement are shown as dotted arrows in the right figure.

What is the minimum number of steps required to go from P2 to P5?

sourceQuestion:
Elvesland is a country that has peculiar beliefs and practices. They express almost all their emotions by gifting flowers. For instance, if anyone gifts a white flower to someone, then it is always taken to be a declaration of one's love for that person. In a similar manner, the gifting of a yellow flower to someone often means that one is angry with that person.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following set(s) of statement(s) can be logically inferred with certainty?

i. In Elvesland, one always declares one's love by gifting a white flower.
ii. In Elvesland, all emotions are declared by gifting flowers.
iii. In Elvesland, sometimes one expresses one's anger by gifting a flower that is not yellow.
iv. In Elvesland, sometimes one declares one's love by gifting a white flower.

A. only (i)
B. (i), (ii) and (iii)
C. (iii), (iii) and (iv)
D. only (iv)

parsed.stem:
Elvesland is a country that has peculiar beliefs and practices. They express almost all their emotions by gifting flowers. For instance, if anyone gifts a white flower to someone, then it is always taken to be a declaration of one's love for that person. In a similar manner, the gifting of a yellow flower to someone often means that one is angry with that person.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following set(s) of statement(s) can be logically inferred with certainty?

i. In Elvesland, one always declares one's love by gifting a white flower.
ii. In Elvesland, all emotions are declared by gifting flowers.
iii. In Elvesland, sometimes one expresses one's anger by gifting a flower that is not yellow.
iv. In Elvesland, sometimes one declares one's love by gifting a white flower.

sourceQuestion:
Out of 1000 individuals in a town, 100 unidentified individuals are covid positive. Due to lack of adequate covid-testing kits, the health authorities of the town devised a strategy to identify these covid-positive individuals. The strategy is to:

i. Collect saliva samples from all 1000 individuals and randomly group them into sets of 5.
ii. Mix the samples within each set and test the mixed sample.
iii. If the test done in (ii) gives a negative result, then declare all 5 individuals to be covid negative.
iv. If the test done in (ii) gives a positive result, then all 5 individuals to be covid positive.

If the test done in (iii) gives a negative result, how many more ______ testing kits will be required to identify the 100 covid-positive individuals irrespective of how they are grouped?

A. 700
B. 600
C. 800
D. 1000

parsed.stem:
Out of 1000 individuals in a town, 100 unidentified individuals are covid positive. Due to lack of adequate covid-testing kits, the health authorities of the town devised a strategy to identify these covid-positive individuals. The strategy is to:

i. Collect saliva samples from all 1000 individuals and randomly group them into sets of 5.
ii. Mix the samples within each set and test the mixed sample.
iii. If the test done in (ii) gives a negative result, then declare all 5 individuals to be covid negative.
iv. If the test done in (ii) gives a positive result, then all 5 individuals to be covid positive.

If the test done in (iii) gives a negative result, how many more ______ testing kits will be required to identify the 100 covid-positive individuals irrespective of how they are grouped?

sourceQuestion:
P, Q, R, S and T have launched a new startup. Two of them are siblings. The office of the startup has just three rooms. All of them agree that the siblings should not share the same room.

If P and Q are single children, and the room allocations shown below are acceptable to all:

  Room PR | Room TS | Room Q
  Room PQ | Room RT | Room S

then, which one of the given options is the siblings?

A. P and T
B. P and S
C. T and Q
D. T and R

parsed.stem:
P, Q, R, S and T have launched a new startup. Two of them are siblings. The office of the startup has just three rooms. All of them agree that the siblings should not share the same room.

If P and Q are single children, and the room allocations shown below are acceptable to all:

  Room PR | Room TS | Room Q
  Room PQ | Room RT | Room S

then, which one of the given options is the siblings?

sourceQuestion:
A recent survey shows that 60% of tobacco users were advised to stop consuming tobacco. The survey also shows that 3 out of 10 tobacco users attempted to stop using tobacco.

Based only on the information in the above passage, which one of the following options can be logically inferred with certainty?

A. A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco made an attempt to do so.
B. A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco did not attempt to do so.
C. Approximately 30% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.
D. Approximately 30% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.
sourceAnswer: B. A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco did not attempt to do so.

parsed.stem:
A recent survey shows that 60% of tobacco users were advised to stop consuming tobacco. The survey also shows that 3 out of 10 tobacco users attempted to stop using tobacco.

Based only on the information in the above passage, which one of the following options can be logically inferred with certainty?

options.text: A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco made an attempt to do so.
A majority of tobacco users who were advised to stop consuming tobacco did not attempt to do so.
Approximately 30% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.
Approximately 30% of tobacco users successfully stopped consuming tobacco.

sourceQuestion:
Students of all the departments of a college who have successfully completed the registration process are eligible to vote in the upcoming college elections. However, by the time the due date for registration was over, it was found that surprisingly none of the students from the Department of Human Sciences had completed the registration process.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following sets of statement(s) can be logically inferred with certainty?

i. All those students who would not be eligible to vote in the college elections would certainly belong to the Department of Human Sciences.
ii. Students from departments other than Human Sciences failed to complete the registration process within the due time.
iii. All the eligible voters would certainly be students who are not from the Department of Human Sciences.

A. (i) and (ii)
B. (i) and (iii)
C. Only (i)
D. Only (iii)
sourceAnswer: D. Only (iii) — All the eligible voters would certainly be students who are not from the Department of Human Sciences.

parsed.stem:
Students of all the departments of a college who have successfully completed the registration process are eligible to vote in the upcoming college elections. However, by the time the due date for registration was over, it was found that surprisingly none of the students from the Department of Human Sciences had completed the registration process.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following sets of statement(s) can be logically inferred with certainty?

i. All those students who would not be eligible to vote in the college elections would certainly belong to the Department of Human Sciences.
ii. Students from departments other than Human Sciences failed to complete the registration process within the due time.
iii. All the eligible voters would certainly be students who are not from the Department of Human Sciences.
